name: Customer declines a quote and leaves a reason
description: Customer taps 'Decline' on the public quote page. They're offered an optional 1-line reason. Reason is saved and visible to the contractor without being stored as customer-facing copy.

persona.name: Sam Browning
persona.context: Got two quotes; this one came in higher. Decided to decline politely.

preconditions: Public quote page loaded for an undecided quote

steps.expect: Lightweight prompt: 'Mind sharing why? (optional)' with chips: Price, Timing, Going elsewhere, Other

steps.action: Taps 'Price' and types 'Got a lower bid', then submits
steps.expect: Page shows a brief 'Thanks — got it' state; no guilt-trip copy

steps.action: Backend marks quote decided=lost with reason metadata
steps.expect: /quotes shows the quote in decided-lost lane; reason visible to contractor on the quote detail
Subtle (no urgent push); appears in the activity feed as 'Browning declined — price'

evaluationCriteria.check: Decline path is accessible without making the customer dig
No emotional manipulation ('Are you sure?'); declining is fast and respectful
Reason chip + freetext are both stored; analytics aggregate top reasons
Reason chips are keyboard-navigable; freetext field is labelled and optional
